task_completion.description: Log in to Rocket.Chat as admin
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rocket.Chat main dashboard is visible in the browser, indicating a successful login.
The channel '#release-updates' is selected in the left sidebar and its chat view is visible.
The 'Room Info' or 'Edit' panel for the channel is open, typically on the right side of the screen.

task_completion.description: Convert the channel from public to private
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Private' toggle is switched on in the settings, or a lock icon appears next to the channel name in the header/sidebar.
The channel name input field shows 'release-updates-internal', or the updated name is visible in the channel header/sidebar.

task_completion.description: Change the channel topic to 'CONFIDENTIAL - Security Remediation'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The topic input field shows 'CONFIDENTIAL - Security Remediation', or the new topic is displayed in the channel header.

integrity.description: Agent used the Rocket.Chat web interface to make changes
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the browser and Rocket.Chat UI, not terminal windows executing curl or mongo commands.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Rocket.Chat application
integrity.visual_evidence: The changes are made within the live Rocket.Chat web application, not typed into a text editor or a mocked HTML page.

integrity.description: Agent did not use automated scripts to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: No code editor or terminal running Python/Bash scripts to hit the Rocket.Chat API is visible.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target original channel name: 'release-updates'. Target new channel name: 'release-updates-internal'. Target topic: 'CONFIDENTIAL - Security Remediation'. Target room type: Private (represented by a lock icon in the UI).
